What is a proton?: A proton is a positively charged particle found in the nucleus of an atom.
What is a neutron?: A neutron is a neutral particle found in the nucleus of an atom.
What is an electron?: An electron is a negatively charged particle that orbits the nucleus of an atom.
What is the speed of light in vacuum?: The speed of light in vacuum is approximately 299,792 kilometers per second.
What is mass?: Mass is a measure of the amount of matter in an object.
What is weight?: Weight is the force exerted on an object due to gravity.
What is temperature?: Temperature is a measure of how hot or cold something is.
What is density?: Density is the mass of a substance per unit volume.
What is an element?: An element is a pure substance made of only one type of atom.
What is a compound?: A compound is a substance formed when two or more elements chemically combine.
What is the pH scale?: The pH scale measures how acidic or basic a substance is.
What is an acid?: An acid is a substance that releases hydrogen ions in solution.
What is a base in chemistry?: A base is a substance that can accept hydrogen ions or release hydroxide ions.
What is a chromosome?: A chromosome is a structure that carries genetic information in living organisms.
What is a gene?: A gene is a segment of DNA that contains instructions for building proteins.
What is a virus?: A virus is a microscopic infectious agent that can only reproduce inside living cells.
What is a bacteria?: A bacterium is a single-celled microorganism.
What is the human brain?: The brain is the central organ of the nervous system and controls most body functions.
What is the human nervous system?: The nervous system is the network that transmits signals throughout the body.
What is the largest bone in the human bo: The femur is the largest bone in the human body.
What is the smallest bone in the human b: The stapes in the middle ear is the smallest bone in the human body.
What is a solar eclipse?: A solar eclipse occurs when the Moon passes between Earth and the Sun.
What is a lunar eclipse?: A lunar eclipse occurs when Earth passes between the Sun and the Moon.
What is a comet?: A comet is a small icy object that orbits the Sun.
What is an asteroid?: An asteroid is a rocky object that orbits the Sun.
What is a nebula?: A nebula is a giant cloud of gas and dust in space.
What is a constellation?: A constellation is a recognized pattern of stars in the night sky.
What is a meteor?: A meteor is the streak of light produced when a meteoroid enters Earth's atmosphere.
What causes the seasons?: The seasons are caused by Earth's axial tilt as it orbits the Sun.
Why is the sky blue?: The sky appears blue because Earth's atmosphere scatters blue wavelengths of sunlight more than other colors.
What is the process of plants turning li: The process is called photosynthesis, where plants use sunlight, water, and carbon dioxide to create oxygen and energy in the form of sugar
Who created the theory of relativity?: The theory of relativity was developed by Albert Einstein.

What is the closest star to Earth?: The Sun is the closest star to our planet

What is the boiling point of water?: Water boils at 100 degrees Celsius at sea level
What is the largest planet in our solar: Jupiter is the largest planet in the solar system
How old is the universe?: The universe is estimated to be around 13.8 billion years old
How many bones are in the human body?: An adult human body has 206 bones
What is gravity?: Gravity is the force by which a planet or other body draws objects toward its center

Which planet is known as the Red Planet?: Mars is known as the Red Planet due to its reddish appearance

What is evolution?: Evolution is the process by which species change over generations.
What is a cell?: A cell is the basic structural and functional unit of life.

What is a molecule?: A molecule is a group of atoms bonded together.
What is chemistry?: Chemistry is the study of matter and its interactions.
What is physics?: Physics is the study of matter, energy, motion, and forces.
What is biology?: Biology is the study of living organisms.
What is astronomy?: Astronomy is the study of celestial objects and the universe.

What is a galaxy?: A galaxy is a vast collection of stars, gas, dust, and dark matter.
What is the Milky Way?: The Milky Way is the galaxy that contains our solar system.
Who was Albert Einstein?: Albert Einstein was a physicist known for the theory of relativity.
Who was Isaac Newton?: Isaac Newton was a physicist and mathematician who formulated the laws of motion and gravity.
